entry.title: NY Times Books: 22 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: NY Times Books: 22 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 16:53PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Inside the Push to Diversify the Book Business
  • The 25 Most Significant New York City Novels From the Last 100 Years
  • ‘An Immense World’ Is a Thrilling Tour of Nonhuman Perception
  • Review: “Resistance: The Underground War Against Hitler, 1939-1945,” by Halik Kochanski
  • Read Your Way Through Stockholm
  • The Poet Who Taught Me to Be in Love With the World
  • Newly Published, From Cookie Stores to Jean Rhys
  • Book Review: “Tree Thieves,” by Lyndsie Bourgon
  • Book Review: “The Long Answer,” by Anna Hogeland
  • Finding Traces of Harriet Tubman on Maryland’s Eastern Shore
  • Review: ‘Alindarka’s Children,’ by Alhierd Bacharevic
  • Book Review: “Learning to Talk,” by Hilary Mantel
  • ‘The Last Resort’ Interrogates the Beach While Enjoying It
  • Boston Revisits ‘Common Ground’ and Busing, Onstage
  • Book Review: “Daddy Issues: Love and Hate in the Time of Patriarchy,” by Katherine Angel
  • Review: ‘Even the Darkest Night,’ by Javier Cercas
  • Review: ‘A Year to the Day,’ by Robin Benway
  • Book Review: ‘Lapvona,’ by Ottessa Moshfegh
  • Building Back Better — One Community at a Time
  • The Life of Jean Rhys, a Uniquely Brilliant and Thorny Writer
  • ‘Legends of Drag: Queens of a Certain Age’ Features Drag Elders
  • Review: “Daughters of the Flower Fragrant Garden,” Zhuqing Li
  • Nutcrackers, Sadism and Accounting
  • Book Review: “The Catch” by Alison Fairbrother
  • Jackie, Before Marrying Jack
The latest book reviews, best sellers, news and features from The NY TImes critics and reporters.

entry.title: London Review of Books: 23 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: London Review of Books: 23 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-22 15:58PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • David Trotter - Lolly Willowes  / Valentine Ackland: A Transgressive Life
  • Letters
  • Jacqueline Rose and Sam Frears - You haven’t got your sister pregnant, have you?
  • Julian Bell - At the British Museum: ‘The World of Stonehenge’
  • Deborah Friedell - The Family Roe: An American Story
  • Mike Jay - Driving with Strangers: What Hitchhiking Tells Us about Humanity
  • Thomas Meaney - Free: Coming of Age at the End of History
  • Rachel Nolan - Operation Car Wash: Brazil’s Institutionalised Crime and the Inside Story of the Biggest Corruption Scandal in History
  • John-Paul Stonard - At the Barbican: ‘Postwar Modern’
  • Clare Bucknell - Trespasses
  • Michael Wood - At the Movies: ‘Top Gun: Maverick’
  • Bee Wilson - Planet Palm: How Palm Oil Ended Up in Everything – and Endangered the World  / Oil Palm: A Global History
  • James Butler - Short Cuts: Love of the Gardenesque
  • Fara Dabhoiwala - Freedom Seekers: Escaping from Slavery in Restoration London
  • Jorie Graham - Poem: ‘Fog’
  • Gavin Francis - The Sleeping Beauties and Other Stories of Mystery Illness
  • Richard Shone - Diary: Lydia Lopokova’s Portraits
Literary review publishing essay-length book reviews and topical articles on politics, literature, history, philosophy, science and the arts by leading writers and thinkers

entry.title: Asian Review of Books: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: Asian Review of Books: 21 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-21 23:00PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • “The Kushnameh: The Persian Epic of Kush the Tusked” by Iranshah ibn Abu’l-Khayr
  • “Celia, Misoka, I” by Xue Yiwei
  • New Book Announcement: “China’s Millennial Digital Generation: Conversations with Balinghou (Post-1980s) Indie Filmmakers” by Karen Ma
  • Jacques Offenbach’s “Pomme d’Api” at the French May: a Gallery
  • “Beethoven in Beijing: Stories from the Philadelphia Orchestra’s Historic Journey to China” by Jennifer Lin
  • New Book Announcement: “Akira Kurosawa and Modern Japan” by David A Conrad
  • Podcast with James Uden and Daniel Majchrowicz about “Worlds of Knowledge in Women’s Travel Writing”
  • “The Cabinet” by Kim Un-su
  • “The History and Culture of Iran and Central Asia: From the Pre-Islamic to the Islamic Period”, edited by DG Tor and Minoru Inaba
  • “Fish Swimming in Dappled Sunlight” by Riku Onda
In addition to reviewing books about or of relevance to Asia, the Asian Review of Books also features long-format essays by leading Asian writers and thinkers, to providing an unparalleled forum for discussion of key contemporary issues by Asians for Asia and a vehicle of intellectual depth and breadth where leading thinkers can write on the books, arts and ideas of the day. Widely quoted and referenced, with an archive of more than one thousand book reviews, it is the only web resource dedicated to Asian books. And now, with the addition of the new premium content, the Asian Review of Books, is a must-read publication.

entry.title: Five Books: 21 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Myisha Cherry on Anger at Racial Injustice Books
  • Darren Collins on The Best Finance Books for Teens and Young Adults
  • Elizabeth Laird on The Best Historical Fiction: The 2022 Walter Scott Prize Shortlist
  • David George Haskell on Natural History Books
  • Gary Gerstle on Neoliberalism Books
  • Helen Scales on The Best Ocean Novels for 10-14 Year Olds
  • Jan Loeys on Market Concentration Books
  • Rosa Rankin-Gee on The Best Near-Future Dystopias
  • Cal Flyn on The Notable Novels of Summer 2022
  • Benno Gammerl on Queer History Books
  • Matthew Brookes & Zack Raffin on Wanderlust Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Spring 2022
  • Alison King on Best Verse Novels for 8-12 Year Olds
  • Sue Rainsford on Literary Horror Books
  • Howard Smith on Antitrust Books
  • Carole Hillenbrand on The Best History Books: the 2022 Wolfson Prize Shortlist
  • Mark Webber on NATO Books
  • Wajahat Ali on The Best South Asian American Novels
  • Chris Power on The Best Literary Thrillers
  • Michele Cobb on The Best Audiobooks: the 2022 Audie Awards
  • Serhii Plokhy on Ukraine and Russia Books
  • Sophie Roell on Notable Nonfiction of Early 2022
  • Cal Flyn on Notable Novels of Spring 2022
  • Nigel Warburton on The Best Philosophy Books of 2021
  • Jeffrey Archer on The Best Detective Fiction
  • Marion Turner on Best Medieval Historical Fiction
  • Tanya Selvaratnam on Domestic Violence Books
Expert book recommendations
